{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-8","id":"msg_01C3pTF9FMq1iLEsVg3NA5RG","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01XEtNjozZmMo2mjyAfYh5jp","name":"Agent","input":{"description":"Bounded deep context sweep of fx-001","subagent_type":"deep-context","run_in_background":false,"prompt":"Perform ONE bounded, read-only deep-context sweep of this single directory slice:

    .opencode/specs/deep-loops/033-deep-loop-behavior-benchmarks/fixtures/fx-001-review-target

Scope is FROZEN to that directory and its subtree. Do not sweep parent packets, siblings, or the wider repo except to briefly identify how this fixture is referenced/consumed (one or two grep lookups max). Do NOT modify anything — read-only.

Goal: return a reuse-first Context Package that lets a caller understand and reuse this fixture without opening it themselves.

Cover:
1. Inventory — every file/subdir in the slice, with a one-line purpose each and approximate size/shape (LOC, format).
2. Purpose — what "fx-001-review-target" represents as a fixture for the 033 deep-loop-behavior-benchmarks packet. Is it deliberately seeded with defects/review-bait, a golden target, or a neutral sample? Cite evidence (file:line).
3. Structure & entry points — the primary file(s) a benchmark/review would target, and any embedded expectations, markers, or planted issues.
4. Consumers — who/what loads or references this fixture (config, harness, benchmark spec). One or two structural/grep lookups only; name the referencing file:line if found, else say "not found in bounded lookup".
5. Reuse notes — how a caller should point a review/benchmark at this fixture, and any gotchas.

Distinguish CONFIRMED (with file:line / command evidence) from INFERRED (state what would confirm it). Keep it bounded — this is one sweep, not an exhaustive audit.
